settings.text: Every Pet Deserves to Feel Great

Pawlish started because we believed grooming should be a positive experience for every pet — not something to dread. We’re certified in fear-free handling, use only organic products, and treat every animal that comes through our doors like family.

settings.name: Casey Morgan
settings.bio: Certified master groomer with 10 years of experience. Casey founded Pawlish after working in veterinary clinics and seeing how stressful grooming was for most pets.

settings.name: Jordan Lee
settings.bio: Specializes in large breed dogs and coat rehabilitation. Fear-free certified and incredibly patient with anxious pets.

settings.name: Mia Santos
settings.bio: Our dedicated cat groomer. Trained in feline-specific handling and the only groomer in Madison certified for cat lion cuts.

settings.title: Fear-Free Certified
settings.description: All our groomers are trained in fear-free handling. We work at your pet’s pace and never force anything.

settings.title: Organic Products
settings.description: We use only organic, hypoallergenic shampoos and conditioners. Safe for sensitive skin and the environment.

settings.title: Clean Facility
settings.description: Our salon is cleaned and sanitized between every appointment. Vaccination records required for all pets.

settings.question: What certifications do your groomers hold?
settings.answer: All groomers are certified through the National Dog Groomers Association of America (NDGAA) and trained in Fear Free® handling. Casey holds a Certified Master Groomer designation.

settings.question: What products do you use?
settings.answer: We use organic, cruelty-free, hypoallergenic products. Our core line is plant-based and free from parabens, sulfates, and artificial fragrances. We also carry medicated options for pets with skin conditions.

settings.question: Is your facility climate-controlled?
settings.answer: Yes. The salon is fully climate-controlled year-round and uses HEPA filtration. Each grooming station has its own ventilation and drying area.

settings.question: Do you groom puppies and kittens?
settings.answer: Yes! We recommend starting grooming early to help pets get comfortable with the process. Puppies and kittens must have their first round of vaccinations before visiting.
